summary: Review Azure Automation accounts for published runbook, schedule, webhook, hybrid worker, secure asset, and identity posture that can repurpose trusted operations automation.

targets.takeover_reason: published runbook posture, trigger posture, and automation identity are visible; current identity has visible Automation account or runbook write control

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Keeps the existing operations automation account, modules, assets, and expected maintenance context in place.
capability_steps.boundary: Account posture does not prove any job ran.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can change script logic that operators already expect Azure Automation to run.
capability_steps.boundary: Default output does not print runbook script content.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can preserve the existing invocation path while changing what the runbook does.
capability_steps.boundary: Trigger posture does not prove invocation.

capability_steps.action: reuse automation identity or worker context
capability_steps.api_surface: automation account identity and hybrid workers
capability_steps.downstream_effect: Can run altered automation through already-integrated identity, worker, or secure-asset context.
capability_steps.boundary: Host state, job output, and secure asset values are not collected.

capability_steps.downstream_effect: Normal cover stories include patching, remediation, cleanup, and scheduled maintenance script updates.
capability_steps.boundary: Cover story is not an intent claim.

current_identity_context.name: azurefox-lab-sp
current_identity_context.summary: Current foothold `azurefox-lab-sp` has visible Automation account or runbook write control.

current_state.posture: 6 published runbook(s); runbook types PowerShell, Python3; schedule or webhook trigger posture; managed identity posture; hybrid worker posture; consequence types run-recurring-execution, reintroduce-config, consume-secret-backed-deployment-material

not_collected_by_default.name: runbook script content
not_collected_by_default.reason: The live helper reports safe runbook type and trigger posture by default; content-derived command/resource clues require a narrower review path and raw script bodies are not printed.

not_collected_by_default.name: secure asset values
not_collected_by_default.reason: Automation credentials, certificates, connections, and encrypted variables are not safe default output.

not_collected_by_default.name: job output and status history
not_collected_by_default.reason: Management-plane posture cannot prove a changed runbook executed or what it produced.

not_collected_by_default.name: hybrid worker host state
not_collected_by_default.reason: Automation account posture cannot prove guest-side host impact without worker or host evidence.

not_collected_by_default.name: activity history
not_collected_by_default.reason: Broad Automation history pulls are not needed for default posture and should be a narrow follow-up for timing or actor proof.

targets.summary: account "aa-hybrid-prod" ranks 5/5 for Automation resource-hijack posture; 6 published runbook(s); 5 job schedule(s); 2 webhook(s); current identity can modify Automation posture from visible RBAC.
